consumes.artifact: scripts/lib/coordinator.js (specifically _mergeEpicsInBase at coordinator.js:426-493 and cleanupWorktrees at coordinator.js:501-538)
scripts/lib/models.js (TaskStatus enum and dag epic schema)

produces.artifact: Modified scripts/lib/coordinator.js with dag.yaml staging inside _mergeEpicsInBase and pure-disk cleanup
Modified scripts/cli.js with --epic flag on the cleanup subcommand

requirement.title: Integrate commit carries dag.yaml state transition
requirement.description: The coordinator's _mergeEpicsInBase MUST bundle dag.yaml state changes into the integrate commit it produces. Both epic.status = COMPLETED and epic.worktree = null MUST be mutated in the same in-memory save, and specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST be staged with git add before the integrate commit is created. This closes the §2.2 / §6.1 defect where dag.yaml writes persisted as dirty working-tree state across sessions.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a spec with one or more epics in status=completed awaiting merge, When arcforge merge runs and _mergeEpicsInBase processes an epic, Then the in-memory dag mutation MUST set both epic.status = COMPLETED and epic.worktree = null in the same save operation. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1 (Q1=c resolution)

Given _mergeEpicsInBase has produced the mutated dag.yaml on disk, When the integrate commit is created, Then git add specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST be invoked before the commit so that git log -1 --name-only on the integrate commit includes specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.1

Given an integrate commit produced by _mergeEpicsInBase, When its message is inspected, Then the subject MUST remain feat: integrate <epic> epic (no separate chore commit for the dag.yaml update), and the commit MUST include both the files merged from the epic branch and the dag.yaml state change as a single logical unit. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1

Given arcforge merge completes successfully for an epic, When git status is read immediately after, Then specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST NOT appear in the working-tree diff (no M flag), confirming that the coordinator has left no uncommitted drift behind. design.md §Problem — §2.2 / §6.1 resolution

requirement.title: cleanup is a pure disk-and-branch operation
requirement.description: arcforge cleanup MUST NOT mutate specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml under any code path. With fr-cc-001 transferring worktree=null into the merge commit, cleanup's only responsibilities are filesystem removal, git worktree prune, and local branch deletion for fully-merged branches. This removes the drift window that previously existed between cleanup and any subsequent user action.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given cleanup is invoked for one or more completed epics, When the operation returns, Then specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST be byte-identical to its state before the cleanup invocation (no write, no git add, no diff in git status). design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1 (cleanup purified)

Given cleanup processes an epic whose worktree directory exists on disk, When cleanup runs, Then it MUST remove the worktree directory via fs.rmSync AND invoke git worktree prune so the git worktree registry no longer references the removed path. design.md §Problem — §7.2 cleanup scope

requirement.title: cleanup deletes fully-merged epic branches
requirement.description: arcforge cleanup MUST extend its scope to local branch deletion. For every epic it processes, cleanup MUST attempt git branch -d <epic-branch> and MUST succeed silently when the branch is fully-merged to the current branch, MUST skip silently when the branch is not fully-merged (no -D force-delete), and MUST surface per-branch results in the command output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given an epic whose branch has been fully merged into the current branch, When cleanup processes that epic, Then the branch MUST be removed via git branch -d and the branch name MUST appear in the cleanup JSON result under a removed-branches listing. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.2

Given an epic whose branch has uncommitted changes or is not merged into the current branch, When cleanup processes that epic, Then the branch MUST be left intact (no git branch -D, no force), MUST NOT cause the overall cleanup invocation to fail, and MUST be surfaced in the cleanup JSON result under a skipped-branches listing. design.md §Problem — §7.2(a) branch cleanup gap

Given a cleanup invocation that removes N worktrees, When the cleanup command completes, Then the JSON output MUST list the worktrees removed and, as a separate field, the branches removed versus skipped, so that downstream automation can distinguish the two outcomes. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1

requirement.title: cleanup CLI exposes --epic flag consistent with expand and loop
requirement.description: The CLI surface for cleanup MUST accept a --epic <name> flag to select a single epic, aligning with expand --epic and loop --epic. The existing positional [epic_ids...] form MUST remain functional as an alias. Skills SHOULD use the flag form going forward; positional form is retained solely for backward compatibility with existing invocations.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a user or skill invokes arcforge cleanup --spec-id <id> --epic <name>, When the command parses arguments, Then it MUST target only the named epic within the given spec and MUST produce the same result shape as an equivalent positional invocation arcforge cleanup --spec-id <id> <name>. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.2 (Q3=a)

Given the arcforge CLI help output, When scripts/cli.js is invoked with --help or its usage banner printed, Then the cleanup usage line MUST list both the positional [epic_ids...] form and the --epic <name> flag form. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1

Given arcforge cleanup is invoked with both positional epic ids and the --epic flag, When the CLI parses arguments, Then it MUST error with a clear message that the two forms are mutually exclusive, rather than silently preferring one over the other. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1
